preview_text:
The model favors South Korea at 47% to win, while Czech Republic sits at 23%. The underdog's chance reflects a meaningful gap, though not a dismissal; both teams' non-loss probabilities remain substantial given the 31% draw likelihood.

The spread across outcomes suggests a moderately competitive match. South Korea's win probability leads by roughly a quarter relative to Czech Republic's, but the draw sits close to one-third of all outcomes. The distribution indicates neither a heavily tilted affair nor a coin flip—competitive equilibrium exists, but with a lean toward the favorite.

The model and market diverge notably here. The model assigns South Korea 11 percentage points more win probability than sportsbooks and Polymarket do, while those consensus sources rate Czech Republic roughly 10 points higher. All three sources converge on draw probability near 31%. The dashboard does not adjudicate which assessment of the favorite is correct, only that substantive disagreement exists between the statistical model and real-money pricing.
